LAW AND MOTION 301, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT -APPEARANCE: DEFENDANT LYDA BROOK CAMERON IN PROPRIA PERSONA (665-5472) DEMURRING PARTY NO APPEARANCE BY PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY. MS. CAMERON WAS ADMINISTERED THE OATH UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY AND TESTIFIED THAT SHE GAVE NOTICE OF INTENT TO ARGUE THE TENTATIVE RULING ON DECEMBER 24, 2001 BETWEEN 3:25 P.M. TO 3:30 P.M. BY LEAVING A MESSAGE ON COUNSEL DENISE A. LEADBETTER'S PHONE ANSWERING MACHINE. THE COURT HAVING BEEN SATISFIED THAT DUE NOTICE WAS GIVEN TO THE OPPOSITION ALLOWED MS. CAMERON TO ARGUE THE MATTER. HAVING HEARD ARGUMENT THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS TO BOTH DEMURRERS ON CALENDAR. 1. DEFENDANT CAMERON'S DEMURRER OVERRULED WITH 5 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER OR OTHERWISE PLEAD. 2. DEFENDANT JOHNSTON'S DEMURRER OVERRULED WITH 5 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSSER OR OTHERWISE PLEAD. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE. REPORTED BY ERIC THRONE. CSR 7855

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-19-2002 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-14-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Scott Bonagofsky, Esq., 415-442-1369, appeared on behalf of defendant and moving party Kim Graser. Ron Schivo, Esq., 415-956-8100, appeared on behalf of plaintiff and opposing party Elizabeth Kerbleski.

After argument the matter was taken under submission. The court will rule and put its ruling on the tentative ruling line at 3:00 p.m. this afternoon, Thursday, February 14, 2002. (JJM/MD) Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2002 Continued To Feb-14-02 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. Plaintiff Elizabeth Kerbleski's motion for summary judgment/adjudication was continued to February 14, 2002 per request of the moving party. (JJM) Eric L. Throne, CSR 7855

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2002 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-26-2001 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCE:
Defendant LYDA BROOK CAMERON in propria persona (665-5472) demurring party

No appearance by Plaintiff and opposing party. Ms. Cameron was administered the oath under penalty of perjury and testified that she gave notice of intent to argue the tentative ruling on December 24, 2001 between 3:25 p.m. to 3:30 p.m. by leaving a message on counsel Denise A. Leadbetter's phone answering machine. The Court having been satisfied that due notice was given to the opposition allowed Ms. Cameron to argue the matter. Having heard argument the Court adopted its tentative ruling as to both demurrers on calendar.
1. Defendant CAMERON's demurrer overruled with 5 days leave to answer or otherwise plead.
2. Defendant JOHNSTON's demurrer overruled with 5 days leave to ansser or otherwise plead.
(JJM/md)

Off Calendar Dec-26-2001 Text Ruling

Having heard argument the Court adopted its tentative ruling as to both demurrers on calendar.
